Video.summary: The economy and the markets are under surveillance as the latest developments in finance, economics and investment is covered; Tom Keene, Paul Sweeney, Lisa Mateo, Michael Barr and other guests create some intriguing conversation.
Video.title: Bloomberg Surveillance Radio

Video.summary: Bloomberg's Alix Steel and Paul Sweeney harness the power of Bloomberg Intelligence to provide in-depth research and data on more than 2,000 companies and 130 industries.
Video.title: Bloomberg Intelligence

Video.summary: Focusing on politics and policies on Capitol Hill, in President Trump's administration, the federal agencies and the courts.
Video.title: Balance of Power

Video.summary: Carol Massar, David Gura. Bloomberg Television takes you behind the scenes of our award winning magazine "Bloomberg BusinessWeek"; Carol Massar and David Gura talk with the writers who break news on a weekly basis and get a lighter touch from the editor who oversees the Etc. section.
Video.title: Bloomberg Businessweek

Video.summary: Gold built South Africa, but mining kills some who dig for it; in Stilfontein, a disaster that trapped miners underground reveals how a quest for gold became a story of hunger, survival and death.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: A police raid in a small Philippine town reveals a young mayor's alleged links to a $2 billion Singapore money laundering scandal and Chinese espionage.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: A police raid in a small Philippine town reveals a young mayor's alleged ties to a $2 billion Singapore money laundering scandal and Chinese espionage.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: How an Arizona woman’s house became a gateway for North Korea’s IT operatives, an underground laptop farm that infiltrated US companies and pumped millions to the regime.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Scientists and entrepreneurs are testing radical ideas like solar radiation management and ocean fertilization; they are finding funding for them.
Video.title: Episode 4

Video.summary: The idea of flying cars has largely remained science fiction, until now; a new generation of electrified aircraft is attracting the attention of investors and venture capitalists around the world.
Video.title: Episode 5

Video.summary: Exploring the world of biocomputing; scientists are laying the foundation for a field that may blur the lines between the biological and synthetic.
Video.title: Episode 2

Video.summary: Geothermal has been the underdog of the energy industry, but that may be about to change as new startups use fracking technologies to expand potential geothermal sites.
Video.title: Episode 3

Video.summary: Governments and entrepreneurs are racing to commercialize the moon; these efforts could unlock a new, deep-space economy; but to return and stay will require the highest levels of innovation.
Video.title: Episode 8

Video.summary: South Sudan relies on oil for almost all of its revenue. But some communities living near the oil fields allege polluted water is causing birth defects and miscarriages, raising questions over health and responsibility.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Hidden Cost of South Sudan's Oil Boom
Video.title: The Hidden Cost of South Sudan's Oil Boom

Image.alt: The Hidden Cost of South Sudan's Oil Boom
The Hidden Cost of South Sudan's Oil Boom
The Hidden Cost of South Sudan's Oil Boom

Video.summary: Emily Chang meets Disney Entertainment Co-Chair Dana Walden for an inside look at the entertainment giant's global streaming and TV ambitions.
Video.title: Episode 17

Video.summary: Ji Chaoqun was a young engineer from Beijing heading to the US to study; then China’s spy agency came calling with bags of cash, the promise of glory and an offer that would change his life forever.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission is supposed to root out graft; instead, some of its members have been accused of perpetuating the very wrongdoing they were meant to stop.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: How the son of a key adviser to Iran Supreme Leader Ayatollah Khamenei built a secret oil empire.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Artificial intelligence has begun to exhibit early signs of agency.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Bioacoustics technology and AI are revealing how animals communicate, and may soon allow humans to talk to them.
Video.title: Episode 2

Video.summary: With the promise of unimaginable computing power, a global race for quantum supremacy is raging.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Human survival depends on water, yet global reserves are rapidly shrinking; it's now replaced oil as the most likely cause of global conflict; discussing whether technology can help secure humanity's future water needs.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Pain is a fact of life; for some, it is excruciating and inescapable; but amid the continuing fallout of the opioid crisis, the development of new technologies aims to limit and even eliminate pain.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Soon, another billion people will be online, mostly from developing nations; ways new users will embrace this technology to transform their lives; its meaning for the rest of the world.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Chinese companies dominate the EV battery supply chain and are expanding rapidly outside their home market. Meanwhile, competitors are looking to new chemistries to leapfrog the leading tech.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Emily Chang meets Melinda French Gates in Seattle to discuss how she's navigating her next stage as a philanthropist and investor.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Brazil's largest bank robbery; at the heart of the brazen heist is South America's most dangerous gang and its raid on what is known as the Night of Terror.
Video.title: Episode 1
